sourceMovie.title: Evangelion: 1.0 You Are (Not) Alone
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Audiences latched onto Evangelion: 1.0 You Are (Not) Alone (2007) as a animation theatrical experience: big enough for a group watch, specific enough to reward a second viewing once you know where it's headed. The animation and science fiction blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Evangelion: 2.0 You Can (Not) Advance
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Evangelion: 1.0 You Are (Not) Alone clicked for you, Evangelion: 2.0 You Can (Not) Advance (2009) is a strong next stop: both trade in animation and science fiction, with earned warmth without mush. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Evangelion: 1.0 You Are (Not) Alone?
faq.answer: Try Evangelion: 2.0 You Can (Not) Advance, Evangelion: 3.0+1.0 Thrice Upon a Time, Evangelion: 3.0 You Can (Not) Redo—each shares DNA with Evangelion: 1.0 You Are (Not) Alone while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Evangelion: 1.0 You Are (Not) Alone?
faq.answer: Evangelion: 1.0 You Are (Not) Alone (2007) is best described as Animation, Science Fiction, Action, Drama. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Evangelion: 1.0 You Are (Not) Alone part of a franchise?
faq.answer: Yes—it's part of the "Rebuild of Evangelion Collection" storyline. You can enjoy it standalone, but sequels and tie-ins build on characters and continuity established here.
